skill.name: elevenlabs-music
skill.description: Create original music from text prompts with the inference.sh CLI and the ElevenLabs music app. The skill provides prompt patterns, duration controls, and workflows for background music, scores, jingles, and game audio.
skill.summary: Generate AI music from text prompts using ElevenLabs through inference.sh.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external commands, hardcoded URLs, and weak cryptography matches. Review confirms the CLI and network findings are expected for an inference.sh music-generation skill, while the weak cryptography matches are false positives at the cited lines. Publish with a warning that prompts and generated media may be sent to external inference.sh or ElevenLabs services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Sends Requests to Music Services
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to run infsh app commands for ElevenLabs music generation. This is the intended function, but it executes a local CLI and can transmit prompt text and job data to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show infsh app run commands with prompt payloads. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but the external execution and service call are confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Related Skill Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The related-skills section suggests npx skills add commands. These commands are optional documentation, but running npx can retrieve and execute package tooling from an external registry.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are visible in the cited block. Risk is moderate because they are examples rather than required automatic execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill links to inference.sh, a hosted image, and raw GitHub install instructions. These links are expected documentation resources, but they confirm external network dependencies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain explicit HTTPS URLs. They support the skill purpose and do not show credential exfiltration or hidden endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited lines contain front matter, a parameter table, and prompt documentation. No hashing, encryption, password handling, or cryptographic algorithm usage was found at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of the cited lines found no cryptographic code or algorithm names. The static blocker appears to be a pattern collision in prose or Markdown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Examples Invoke infsh app run
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains bash examples that invoke the inference.sh CLI with user-defined music prompts. Users should review prompt contents before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands and prompt payloads are explicit in the cited blocks. The pattern is expected but materially relevant to security review.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Installation Suggestions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill suggests npx commands for adding related skills. Users should verify package sources before running these optional commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The optional npx commands are present and externally sourced. They are not hidden or automatically executed by the skill.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast original music without switching tools or learning audio production workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create music through inference.sh and ElevenLabs prompts.
actual_capabilities: Runs ElevenLabs music generation through the inference.sh CLI.
Uses text prompts to describe genre, mood, instruments, tempo, and style.
Supports duration settings from 5 seconds to 600 seconds.
Provides examples for background music, cinematic scores, short clips, and game audio.
Shows a workflow for combining generated music with voiceover and media merging.
Lists related audio skills for sound effects, text to speech, and broader music generation.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Sends prompt text and generation requests to external services.
Does not perform local audio editing, mastering, or mixing by itself.
Commercial usage depends on current provider terms and user account rights.

use_cases.title: Create Video Background Tracks
use_cases.description: Generate short or medium-length background music for product videos, tutorials, and social clips.

use_cases.title: Prototype Game Audio
use_cases.description: Draft battle themes, menu loops, and exploration music before commissioning final assets.

use_cases.title: Produce Podcast Branding
use_cases.description: Create intro music, outro music, and segment stingers with consistent mood and duration.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Background Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a general-purpose music bed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60 second upbeat background track for a product demo. Use clean electronic sounds and a positive mood.

prompt_templates.title: Genre and Instrument Control
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with clearer musical direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90 second smooth jazz track with saxophone lead, walking bass, brushed drums, and a relaxed evening mood.

prompt_templates.title: Timed Content Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple assets with different durations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make three music options for a podcast: a 10 second intro, a 5 second segment transition, and a 20 second outro. Keep one consistent sonic identity.

prompt_templates.title: Production Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that combines music with voiceover planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate soft narration background music for a 60 second product tour, then plan how to combine it with a separate voiceover file.

output_examples.input: A 60 second lo-fi study track with mellow piano and vinyl texture.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the music generation workflow and returns a generated audio link with the selected duration and prompt summary.

output_examples.input: A 10 second professional podcast intro for a technology show.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a short branded music cue and reports the generated media location for review.

output_examples.input: A 120 second fantasy exploration theme with harp, flute, and a calm mood.
output_examples.output: The assistant generates a longer game-ready draft track and summarizes the musical direction used.

best_practices: Include genre, mood, instruments, tempo, and intended use in each prompt.
Start with short drafts before generating longer tracks for final review.
Review provider terms before using generated music in commercial projects.
anti_patterns: Do not include private scripts, unreleased lyrics, or confidential campaign details in prompts.
Do not run optional installation commands without verifying the source package.
Do not expect the skill to mix, master, or edit audio without another tool.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates original music from text prompts through the inference.sh CLI and the ElevenLabs music app.

faq.question: Do I need an external account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need the inference.sh CLI installed and authenticated before generation commands can run.

faq.question: Can I control the length of the music?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented duration setting supports 5 to 600 seconds.

faq.question: Can I specify genre and instruments?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill encourages prompts with genre, mood, instruments, tempo, and style.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to external services?
faq.answer: Yes. Music prompts and generation requests are sent through inference.sh and related music services.

faq.question: Is the generated music ready for commercial use?
faq.answer: The skill describes commercial use, but users should confirm current provider terms and account rights before publishing.
